Fast forward nearly three decades. Extrachromosomal circular DNA (eccDNA) is not unique to yeast. Similar circular DNA molecules appear in mammalian cells, aging tissues, and cancers

Why does this matter?

Because recombination events that generate ERCs originate from DNA damage and double-strand breaks (DSBs) at the rDNA locus
